title: ADAS EdTech
summary: Completed overview of ADAS EdTech as training/education solutions for the Advanced Driver-Assistance Systems workforce. Mapped major players (I-CAR, ASE, equipment-vendor academies, simulation/VR providers), summarized 2025–2026 trends (calibration complexity, apprenticeship expansion, credential standardization, immersive CTE), and highlighted opportunities/risks for business stakeholders. Answer-only deliverable; no file artifacts created.
assumptions: ADAS EdTech refers to education and training technology for the ADAS ecosystem (technicians, collision repair, calibration, and engineering validation), not a single named company or K-12 product category.
Geographic scope weighted toward North America and Europe where recent news coverage is strongest; Asia-Pacific ADAS adoption noted as demand driver but with less EdTech-specific sourcing.
Market sizing figures from consultancy press releases are treated as directional estimates, not audited financials.
Overview depth prioritizes business positioning over technical calibration procedures.
